claim: Software 3.0 shifts from explicit code (1.0) and trained weights (2.0) to prompting LLMs as the primary programming interface
actionable: Reframe agent development as 'what text do I copy-paste to my agent' rather than writing imperative code; let the LLM handle implementation details
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: Agentic engineering is distinct from vibe coding: it's about coordinating fallible, stochastic agents while preserving professional software quality bars
actionable: When building with agents, maintain code quality standards (no vulnerabilities, no security regressions) while leveraging agent speed; you remain responsible for agent output
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: LLMs exhibit 'jaggedness'—extreme peaks in verifiable domains (math, code) and troughs in non-verifiable tasks, shaped by RL reward signals during training
actionable: When using LLMs, expect strong performance on verifiable tasks (code refactoring, chess) but guard against failures on seemingly simple tasks (distance reasoning); test edge cases
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: Model capabilities improve disproportionately in domains present in training data; chess improved 10x from GPT-3.5 to GPT-4 primarily due to increased chess dataset inclusion
actionable: When evaluating LLM capability for your domain, infer pre-training distribution coverage; if your domain is underrepresented, plan for fine-tuning or prompting workarounds
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: Think of LLMs as 'ghosts' not 'animals': statistical simulation circuits without intrinsic motivation, requiring taste and judgment to direct effectively
actionable: When prompting agents, focus on specifying desired output state and constraints rather than trying to align with intrinsic agent motivation; you provide the direction
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: You can outsource thinking but cannot outsource understanding; maintaining deep comprehension of agent behavior is non-delegable to prevent costly errors
actionable: Even with autonomous agents writing code, keep detailed understanding of architecture, design decisions, and system invariants; oversee agent outputs critically
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: Agents are poor at aesthetic choices, code simplification, and design elegance; guide these explicitly as they are outside RL reward functions
actionable: For code quality beyond functional correctness, write detailed design specs for agents or review generated code for bloat, copy-paste, and brittle abstractions
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan

claim: Hiring for agentic engineering should test real multi-day projects with agents, security challenges, and live testing—not competitive programming puzzles
actionable: Evaluate candidates by having them build a substantial system with agents (e.g., a Twitter clone for agents) and test its security, rather than timed algorithm problems
source_title: Andrej Karpathy: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering w/ Stephanie Zhan
